channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Violent clashes give way to peaceful PSG parade after Champions League win
item.description: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.
item.content:encoded:
A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.

(Image credit: Emma Da Silva)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Right-wing candidate pulls ahead in first round of Colombia's presidential vote
item.description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.
item.content:encoded:
Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.

(Image credit: Fernando Vergara)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. bombs Iranian military sites and Kuwait is hit by drone and missile fire
item.description: The U.S. military says it bombed Iranian radar and drone control sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American MQ-1 Predator drone this weekend. Kuwait said its air defenses opened fire on Monday.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military says it bombed Iranian radar and drone control sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American MQ-1 Predator drone this weekend. Kuwait said its air defenses opened fire on Monday.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How aid cuts are hampering the frontline response to the Ebola crisis
item.description: Aid workers in Uganda are watching the Ebola crisis unfold in the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.
item.content:encoded:
Aid workers in Uganda are watching the Ebola crisis unfold in the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.

(Image credit: Leonard Musinguzi)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: The terrifying day the U.S.-Iran war reached a sleepy Sri Lankan town
item.description: A deadly strike during the first days of the Iran war hit far away in the Indian Ocean, jolting a quiet seaside town and showing how far the conflict's reach extends.
item.content:encoded: A deadly strike during the first days of the Iran war hit far away in the Indian Ocean, jolting a quiet seaside town and showing how far the conflict's reach extends.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in Pacific Ocean, in fourth attack this week
item.description: The U.S. military said it carried out another strike on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Pacific Ocean, killing three in the fourth attack this week and putting the total death toll at 205.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military said it carried out another strike on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Pacific Ocean, killing three in the fourth attack this week and putting the total death toll at 205.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel seizes medieval castle as it expands major offensive in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel has captured the 12th-century Beaufort castle that overlooks southern Lebanon and northern Israel. The move suggests Israel is planning an extended military presence in Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: Israel has captured the 12th-century Beaufort castle that overlooks southern Lebanon and northern Israel. The move suggests Israel is planning an extended military presence in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Israel is extending its footprint through war. For some, it's part of a greater plan
item.description: On the fringes of Israel's far right, some activists and political leaders dream of a Greater Israel, extending the country's area of control into neighboring countries.
item.content:encoded: On the fringes of Israel's far right, some activists and political leaders dream of a Greater Israel, extending the country's area of control into neighboring countries.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: PSG wins back-to-back Champions League titles after shootout victory against Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain has won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest.
item.content:encoded:
Paris Saint-Germain has won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest.

(Image credit: Denes Erdos)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says a ceasefire extension deal with Iran is near, but core issues remain
item.description: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf
